site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 13 preview board for tipping, charity betting discipline, and post-Origin team-news edges
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 13 tips, post-Origin late mail, and charity betting discipline.

ticker: round 13 is live with seven games from friday 29 may to sunday 31 may
no charity bet is locked until price stake and final team news are confirmed
cleanest shortlist read is knights over eels with ponga backing up and parramatta missing moses and paulo
broncos over dragons is the strongest raw tip with payne haas named to return
storm versus roosters and panthers versus warriors carry heavy origin back-up risk
sharks versus sea eagles is a friday danger game with roster losses on both sides

featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 13 is about post-Origin recovery, late scratches, and whether named stars actually back up
The cleanest team-news edge is Newcastle over Parramatta because Ponga is set to play while Moses and Paulo are out
Brisbane get a major front-row lift with Payne Haas named to return against a still-thin Dragons side
Penrith versus Warriors is the showcase game, but both sides have major availability questions

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Storm versus Roosters depends on several Origin players being cleared after short turnarounds
Sharks lose Nicho Hynes, Briton Nikora and KL Iro, so the Friday tip should stay modest
Panthers have Cleary, Yeo, McLean and To'o listed as reserves while the Warriors rest Barnett and Capewell
A Round 12 clean sweep is validation of process, not permission to force a Round 13 bet

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Separate tip confidence from charity-bet execution
Wait for final 24-hour and 90-minute updates before turning any shortlist lean into a locked bet
Track whether Origin players are named, trained, and actually kept in the final 17
Keep the ledger frozen unless there is a real stake and price

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Knights price against an Eels spine missing Mitchell Moses
Broncos response with Haas and Piakura back
Whether Matt Burton and Stephen Crichton are managed differently by Canterbury after Origin
Warriors resilience without Roger Tuivasa-Sheck, Mitch Barnett and Kurt Capewell

betCard.title: Round 13 Charity Bet
why: Kalyn Ponga has been confirmed as feeling good and set to back up from Origin One.
Parramatta are missing Mitchell Moses and Junior Paulo, which hits both control and middle-field stability.
The shortlist fits the team-news-first process, but still needs a real price and final team confirmation.
risks: Do not move this into the charity ledger without a recorded stake and odds.
Ponga backing up still carries fatigue and late-management risk.
Newcastle are not a set-and-forget side, so price discipline matters.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.
Rounds 10 and 11: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 12: No charity bet locked; tips went 5 from 5, but the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 13: Shortlist only at preview time; no stake or odds recorded, so the ledger is unchanged.

watchlist: Re-check Knights versus Eels 24-hour teams before using it as a charity play.
Do not count a shortlist lean as a bet unless the stake and odds are recorded before kickoff.
Watch Friday late mail carefully: Sharks and Sea Eagles both carry forced changes.
Storm, Roosters, Panthers and Warriors need extra caution because of Origin back-up and rest decisions.
Preserve the Round 12 validated 5 from 5 result separately from this Round 13 preview.
confidence: Broncos over Dragons - strongest raw side and Haas return
Knights over Eels - cleanest shortlist edge, pending price
Bulldogs over Wests Tigers - steadier structure if Burton backs up
Raiders over Cowboys - home lean with Strange, Young and Kris back
Storm over Roosters - AAMI lean, heavy Origin caveat
Sharks over Sea Eagles - home lean only, high roster-loss risk
Panthers over Warriors - lowest confidence showcase tip

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 13 is a post-Origin week, so team certainty matters more than bravado
lines: As of Friday, 29 May 2026, official NRL team lists and late mail have been checked for Round 13.
The round runs from Sharks versus Sea Eagles on Friday night through Panthers versus Warriors on Sunday evening.
The board is a preview and tip card only; no Mark Hughes Foundation charity bet has been locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Newcastle are the shortlist, not the ledger
lines: Knights over Eels is the cleanest betting watch because Ponga is set to back up while Parramatta lose Mitchell Moses and Junior Paulo.
The edge is not enough by itself: it needs a price, stake, and final team confirmation before kickoff.
If those details are not recorded before the match, the correct ledger action is no bet.

sections.title: Round 13 Fixtures
sections.heading: Official Round 13 fixture board
lines: Sharks v Sea Eagles - Friday 29 May, 8:00pm at Ocean Protect Stadium.
Knights v Eels - Saturday 30 May, 3:00pm at McDonald Jones Stadium.
Wests Tigers v Bulldogs - Saturday 30 May, 5:30pm at CommBank Stadium.
Storm v Roosters - Saturday 30 May, 7:35pm at AAMI Park.
Broncos v Dragons - Sunday 31 May, 2:00pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Raiders v Cowboys - Sunday 31 May, 4:05pm at GIO Stadium.
Panthers v Warriors - Sunday 31 May, 6:15pm at CommBank Stadium.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who gets upgraded or held back this week
lines: Upgrade: Broncos with Payne Haas named, Knights with Ponga confirmed to back up, and Raiders at home with key personnel returning.
Hold: Bulldogs, because Matt Burton is likely but Stephen Crichton is still an extended-bench monitor.
Downgrade: Eels without Moses and Paulo, Sharks without Hynes, and Warriors without Tuivasa-Sheck, Barnett and Capewell.
Volatility bucket: Storm versus Roosters and Panthers versus Warriors until final teams settle.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The late-mail details driving the card
lines: Cronulla are without Nicho Hynes, Briton Nikora and KL Iro; Manly are without Tolu Koula and rest Haumole Olakau'atu.
Newcastle have Ponga backing up, while Parramatta reshape the spine around Ronald Volkman with Moses out.
Brisbane name Payne Haas, Brendan Piakura and several Origin players to back up, while the Dragons still carry injury churn.
Penrith are monitoring Cleary, Yeo, McLean and To'o; the Warriors rest Barnett and Capewell and lose Tuivasa-Sheck for the season.
